concepts.effect: Higher ratio = more speed, more force needed. Lower ratio = easier climbing.
Longer crank arms increase torque at the pedal.
Wider, softer tyres increase rolling resistance but improve grip.
Higher friction coefficient (mu) from better brakes/tyres shortens stopping distance.
Faster spinning wheels resist tipping, aiding balance.
Lower center of gravity improves stability especially cornering.
Cyclist power output drives speed against resistive forces.
Climbing converts kinetic energy to potential energy; descending reverses it.
